title: Yeast DAPI Staining
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"9trdr","text":"Stains the nucleus of budding or fission yeast with DAPI in order to see it with a fluorescence microscope.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Grow up yeast in liquid medium overnight. The OD may not matter too much here, but something in the 0.8 - 2 range is probably ideal.
Add 333 μL (or 1 volume) yeast culture to a 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube. undetermined
Add 666 μL (or 2 volume) of 100% Ethanol to the 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube. undetermined Ethyl alcohol, Pure 200 proof, for molecular biology Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #E7023
Let the yeast ethanol mixture sit at room temperature for 30-60 minutes. 00:30:00
Spin down yeast cells for 1 minute at 2500 RPM. 00:01:00
Pour out the supernatant and resuspend the pellet in 1mL of 1 x PBS, then centrifuge for 1 minute at 2500 RPM. undetermined 1X PBS (Phosphate-buffered saline )
Pour out the supernatant and resuspend the pellet in 200 μL of a 1 x PBS / 1:2000 Dilution DAPI mixture. undetermined PBS / DAPI 1:2000 Dilution Mixture by Alan Cone

Add one drop of the yeast suspended in the PBS / DAPI mixture onto a microscope slide, add a coverslip on top, and go observe the stained yeast. Make sure after you do this part to go look at it within a few hours of resuspending in PBS / DAPI. The sooner you can get to a microscope the better.
creator: Alan Cone
